title: Call and put option structure: A practical guide to investing in foreign-parented start-ups
description: The problem: A regulatory bottleneckIndian Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), the SEBI-regulated vehicles that pool capital from sophisticated investors to invest in start-ups, venture-stage companies, and other alternative assets, have long sought exposure to the best Indian start-ups. In today's ecosystem, many of the most promising Indian start-ups operate through a 'flipped' structure: an Indian operating subsidiary (Indian Opco) is wholly owned by a foreign holding company (referred to he
